title: Aurora Cannabis Acquires Safari Flower Company for EU GMP Capacity Expansion
text: Aurora Cannabis Inc. announced on April 15, 2026, acquisition of Safari Flower Company, established EU GMP certified cannabis cultivator and manufacturer. Deal terms valued at $26.5 million, subject to customary adjustments, inclusive of cash payment of $2 million contingent on satisfaction of certain conditions. Deal involves issuance of 2,417,180 common shares and cash payment of $15 million, with additional $2 million contingent on certain conditions being met. Strategic purpose: Aurora Cannabis acquired Safari Flower Company in $26.5 million cash and stock deal, expanding EU GMP-certified cultivation capacity to supply key international medical cannabis markets including Germany, Australia, Poland, and UK. Acquisition adds 59,000 sq ft EU GMP-certified cultivation and manufacturing facility. Expected benefits: Aurora expects positive Adjusted EBITDA contributions in fiscal 2027 with further benefits in 2028. Acquisition accelerates global medical cannabis leadership strategy and expands manufacturing capacity across regulated international markets. This is significant strategic move expanding Aurora's EU GMP infrastructure to serve growing high-margin international markets with additional cultivation and manufacturing capability.
